title: Modular system and method for controlling subsea operations
abstract: The invention concerns a modular system for controlling subsea operations. The modular system comprises a control room structure at a top side location, the control room structure is provided with a fixed modular connection assembly. The interior of the control room structure is prepared for accommodation of a set of replaceable control modules comprising at least one dedicated control module provided for controlling a corresponding operational well tool. The said set of replaceable control module(s) is selected in accordance with a chosen subsea operation for accommodation in the control room structure. The said set of replaceable control module(s) accommodated in the control room structure is connected to the fixed modular connection assembly for establishing communication between the at least one dedicated control module and its corresponding operational well tool. The invention also concerns a method for controlling subsea operations.
claims:
Claims 1. Modular system for controlling subsea operations, comprising a control room structure at a top side location, the control room structure is provided with a fixed modular connection assembly wherein the interior of the control room structure is prepared for accommodation of a set of replaceable control modules comprising at least one dedicated control module provided for controlling a corresponding operational well tool, and the said set of replaceable control module(s) is selected in accordance with a chosen subsea operation for accommodation in the control room structure, wherein the said set of replaceable control module(s) accommodated in the control room structure is connected to the fixed modular connection assembly for establishing communication between the at least one dedicated control module and its corresponding operational well tool. 2. Modular system for controlling subsea operations, in accordance with claim 1 , wherein the control room structure is arranged with a compartment unit having storage space for the set of replaceable control modules. 3. Modular system for controlling subsea operations, in accordance with claim 2, wherein the compartment unit is movably arranged in the interior of the control room structure. 4. Modular system for controlling work over operations, in accordance with claim 2 or 3, wherein the compartment unit is movably arranged between a storage position wherein at least a portion of the compartment unit is positioned inside the control room structure and an access position providing outside access to the compartment unit. 5. Modular system for controlling subsea operations, in accordance with one of claims 2-4, wherein at least one of the replaceable control module is provided to be movably arranged between a between a storage position where least one of the replaceable control module is essentially accommodated inside the storage space of the compartment unit and an access position where at least a portion of the at least one replaceable control module is positioned outside the storage space of the compartment unit. 6. Modular system for controlling subsea operations, in accordance with one of the preceding claims, wherein the fixed modular connection assembly is provided with at least one connection member with a standardized interface for receiving a 

 connector of at least one line communicating with the operational well tool(s). 7. Modular system for controlling subsea operations, in accordance with one of the preceding claims, wherein the fixed modular connection assembly comprises a hydraulic module and the fixed modular connection assembly is arranged for receiving signals from the replaceable control modules and transferring the signals as pressure signals to the operational well tool(s). 8. Method for controlling subsea operations comprising the following steps: - providing a control room structure from a top side location, - providing the control room structure with fixed modular connection assembly and providing the control room structure with a accommodating unit for receiving and storing a set of replaceable control modules, -selecting the set of replaceable control modules in accordance with a chosen subsea operation, wherein at least one of the replaceable control modules is a dedicated control module provided for controlling a corresponding operational well tool, -arranging the set of replaceable control modules in the accommodating unit, -providing a connection between the set of replaceable control modules and the fixed modular connection assembly for establishing communication between the at least one dedicated control module and its corresponding operational well tool.
